item.title: Brackett — Categorizing Sound
item.description: Author: David Brackett Title: Categorizing Sound: Genre and Twentieth-Century Popular Music Year: 2016 Type: Academic book (University of California Press) A history of the practice of categorizing rather than of any category’s contents.

item.title: Ward — Just My Soul Responding
item.description: Author: Brian Ward Title: Just My Soul Responding: Rhythm and Blues, Black Consciousness, and Race Relations Year: 1998 Type: Academic book (University of California Press) The book opens by pairing two events from 1954: Brown v.

item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Era1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976)GenresFolk rockContemporary folkSinger-songwriterBlues rockCountry rockSceneGreenwich Village folk sceneListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an acoustic guitar a...

item.title: John Lennon
item.description: EraBritish Invasion (with the Beatles, 1963–1970); solo, 1968–1980InstrumentRhythm guitar, vocals, piano, harmonicaGenresRockSinger-songwriterPop rockBandsThe BeatlesPlastic Ono BandListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The most influential rock voice of its generation belonged to a man who could not stand ...

item.title: Muddy Waters
item.description: Era1948–1958 (Chess peak); active to 1983GenresChicago bluesElectric bluesDelta bluesSceneChicago (Chess Records)ListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify The voice is unhurried and half-spoken, sure of itself to the point of menace, a man stating his business and daring the room to disagree.

item.title: The Kinks
item.description: Era1964–1996 (primary period: 1964–1970)GenresPop rockModBaroque popArt rockSceneModNorth LondonListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify Dave Davies slashed his amplifier speaker with a razor blade and the sound that came out — a ragged, distorted roar underpinning the riff on “You Really Got Me” — has a plaus...

item.title: Blues
item.description: Periodc. 1890s–presentNamedc. 1912, with the first published “blues” sheet musicGeographic originAfrican American communities of the U.S.
